palette_hint: Background #F5F0E8 (warm parchment), body text #2C2416 (dark walnut), accent #4A7C59 (forest green) and #C4622D (burnt clay) for buttons and highlights.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif, bold weight, body in system-ui sans-serif at regular weight — grounded and trustworthy, evoking old maps and field notebooks.
description: Hollow Creek Land Trust is a fictional Appalachian nonprofit preserving over 12,000 acres of forested ridgeline and creek-bottom habitat through conservation easements, community partnerships, and volunteer stewardship programs.
constraints: Max page content width is 960px, centered with auto margins and 24px horizontal padding on all pages.
Navigation is a flex row with the logo-wordmark on the left and five nav links on the right; background #2C2416, link text #F5F0E8, active page link underlined in #C4622D.
Footer has a 3-column grid (mission blurb | quick links | contact details) on #4A7C59 background with #F5F0E8 text, 40px top/bottom padding.
All call-to-action buttons use background #C4622D, white text, 8px border-radius, 12px 28px padding — no hover effects.
Section headings use a 2px solid #4A7C59 left border with 12px left padding to create a consistent visual anchor across pages.

page_specs.our-land: The hero section displays a large aspect-ratio box (16:9, background color #4A7C59 with centered white text 'Hollow Creek Watershed — 12,400 Acres Protected') followed by a 3-column CSS grid of property cards, each with a colored rectangle placeholder, property name, acreage, and county. Featured properties include Sycamore Bottoms (840 ac, Clay County), Ridgeback Hollow (1,200 ac, Macon County), and Tuckasegee Bluffs (620 ac, Jackson County).
page_specs.conservation: A two-column flex layout pairs a tall text column (explaining conservation easements, forest management plans, and water-quality monitoring) with a sidebar listing 4 active programs: Riparian Buffer Restoration, Prescribed Fire Partnership, Salamander Monitoring Network, and Working Forest Easements. Below, a full-width stats bar displays three key numbers — 47 easements held, 12,400 acres protected, 18 partner landowners — in large #C4622D numerals on a #2C2416 background.
page_specs.volunteer: An introductory paragraph describes seasonal stewardship opportunities, followed by a 2-column grid of six volunteer role cards (Trail Crew, Invasive Species Removal, Water Quality Sampling, Boundary Monitoring, School Program Educator, Event Support) each with role title, a short 2-sentence description, and time commitment. The page ends with a simple signup call-to-action box with a #C4622D button labeled 'Join the Stewardship Team'.
page_specs.donate: A centered headline 'Your Gift Protects Wild Appalachia' leads into a 3-column giving-level grid showing three tiers — Creek Keeper ($50), Ridge Guardian ($250), and Watershed Protector ($1,000+) — each in a bordered card listing specific impact statements (e.g., '$250 funds one acre of riparian buffer planting'). Below the grid, a plain-styled recurring-gift callout box explains the monthly giving program 'Hollow Creek Circle' with a #C4622D donate button.
page_specs.about-us: A two-column flex section pairs a staff roster (Executive Director Maren Suttle, Conservation Director Elias Trowbridge, Stewardship Coordinator Deja Whitfield, each with title and a 40x40px colored circle avatar placeholder) with a text column covering the organization's founding in 2003 by a coalition of Clay County farmers and environmental educators. Below, a full-width timeline rendered as a horizontal flex row marks milestones: 2003 founding, 2007 first easement, 2014 10,000-acre milestone, 2021 salamander partnership, 2024 new headquarters.
